title: Tapping for "His Money Isn't My Money"
subtitle: A tapping practice for releasing the feeling that money earned by a partner belongs to them, not you, and landing in a settled sense of financial autonomy within a shared life
excerpt: A five-minute tapping practice for the stuck-on feeling that household income sourced through a partner is not really yours to use freely. The script works with the guilt, smallness, and asking-permission dynamic around money, and moves toward autonomy that lives alongside love.
sourceNotes: Original tapping script written for homemade.education. Draws on EFT framework (Gary Craig, mid-1990s) applied to financial autonomy within partnership. Related principles from The Money Zone (Rebecca Page, 2024).

practice.whenToUse: Use when you feel the pull to ask permission before spending, minimise what you spend on yourself, or carry guilt about using household money for your own needs or business.

content.text: A five-minute tapping practice for the feeling that household money sourced through a partner is not fully yours to use. The script addresses the guilt and smallness that sit underneath that feeling, and works toward a settled sense of financial autonomy that does not require earning independently in order to be real.
New to tapping? Read
how EFT tapping works
first.
The practice
Karate chop
Even though part of me feels like it's his money, not mine, I deeply and completely accept myself.
Even though I sometimes feel like I have to justify what I spend, I deeply and completely accept myself.
Even though not earning it myself makes me feel less entitled to it, I deeply and completely accept myself.
Tapping round
Eyebrow: this guilt about spending is not the truth.
Side of eye: I contribute in ways that are real, even when they don't pay.
Under eye: the household income is household income.
Under nose: I am allowed to have money that is mine to decide with.
Chin: I can be loved and autonomous at the same time.
Collarbone: asking permission is not love. It is smallness.
Under arm: I release the habit of making myself small with money.
Top of head: I have always deserved to feel financially whole.
Reframe to positive
Eyebrow: my autonomy is mine, whatever the source.
Side of eye: I use money with confidence and care.
Under eye: I trust myself to make good decisions.
Under nose: I am a full partner in this financial life.
Chin: I can spend on myself without guilt.
Collarbone: love and financial autonomy belong together.
Under arm: I step into the full financial life that is mine.
Top of head: the money in my hands is mine to use well.
Tap each round three times, then move to the reframe and tap that three times.
Where this practice comes from
Written for homemade.education. Uses Gary Craig's EFT tapping framework, in use since the mid-1990s. Related principles from The Money Zone (Rebecca Page, 2024).
